mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 60%+ of periods when FEDFUNDS declines by >0.5% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market demand, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 50%+ of months with PAYEMS growth > 50k.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) signal improved consumer confidence and demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in 40%+ of periods with HOUST growth > 10% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day lagged increase in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2% triggers central bank tightening expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 60%+ of periods with CPIAUCSL > 2% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) signal supply-side shocks and weakening demand, causing SPX 20d returns to fall.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1.5% MoM and UNRATE rises > 0.3pp
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO falls and UNRATE rises.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in real sales (RSAFS) combined with falling GDP (GDPC1) indicates weak consumer demand, leading to negative SPX returns over 60 days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 5% YoY and GDPC1 falls > 1% QoQ
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in 40%+ of periods with RSAFS and GDPC1 declines.

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day lagged drop in unemployment (UNRATE) below 4% signals tight labor markets, boosting corporate profits and generating SPX 5d positive returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 50%+ of periods when UNRATE < 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) amid falling unemployment (UNRATE) indicates strong supply-side capacity and demand, driving SPX 20d returns upward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 1.5% MoM and UNRATE falls > 0.2pp
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO rises and UNRATE falls.
